entries.title: Song of Solomon — Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. A contrast of men's gifts and God's, who, though author of both blessings, confers the latter by His more special providence. and-- or, "but," implying that the evils of Pr 19:13 are only avoided by His care.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. a deep sleep-- a state of utter indifference. idle soul-- or, "person" (compare Pr 10:4; 12:24).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. (Compare Pr 10:17; 13:13). despiseth ... ways-- opposed to keeping or observing, neglects (Pr 16:17) (as unworthy of regard) his moral conduct.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. (Compare Pr 14:21; Psa 37:26). hath pity-- shown by acts (compare Margin).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. (Compare Pr 13:24; 23:13). let not ... spare-- literally, "do not lift up thy soul" (Psa 24:4; 25:1), that is, do not desire to his death; a caution to passionate parents against angry chastisement.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. Repeated efforts of kindness are lost on ill-natured persons.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. (Compare Pr 13:18-20). latter end-- (Pr 5:11). In youth prepare for age.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. (Compare Pr 16:1, 9; Psa 33:10, 11). The failure of man's devices is implied.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. desire-- that is, to do good, indicates a kind disposition (Pr 11:23); and the poor thus affected are better than liars, who say and do not.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. The fear ... life-- (Compare Pr 3:2). abide-- or, "remain contented" (1Ti 4:8). not visited with evil-- (Pr 10:3; Psa 37:25), as a judgment, in which sense visit is often used (Psa 89:32; Jr 6:15).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. bosom-- literally, a wide dish in which the hand was plunged in eating (Mt 26:23). Compare Pr 26:15, the sentiment expressed with equal irony and less exaggeration.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. Such is the benefit of reproof; even the simple profit, much more the wise.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. Unfilial conduct often condemned (Pr 17:21-25; 20:20; De 21:18, 21).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. Avoid whatever leads from truth.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. ungodly witness-- (Compare Margin), one false by bad principles (compare Pr 6:12). scorneth judgment-- sets at naught the dictates of justice. devoureth-- literally, "swalloweth," as something delightful.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. Their punishment is sure, fixed, and ready (compare Pr 3:34; 10:13).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 20 Pr 20:1-30. 1. mocker-- scorner. Such men are made by wine. strong drink-- made by spicing wine (compare Is 5:11, 22); and it may include wine. raging-- or boisterous as a drunkard. deceived-- literally, "erring," or reeling.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 1:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. (Compare Pr 19:12). Men who resist authority injure themselves (Ro 13:2).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. to cease from strife-- or, better, "to dwell from or without strife," denoting the habit of life. fool ... meddling-- (Pr 17:14).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. shall ... beg-- literally, "ask" (in this sense, Psa 109:10).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. Counsel ... water-- that is, deeply hidden (Pr 18:4; Psa 13:2). The wise can discern well.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. Boasters are unreliable. goodness-- or, "kind disposition."

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. The conduct of good men proclaims their sound principles. God's covenant and their good example secure blessing to their children (Pr 4:26; Psa 112:1, 2).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. As in Pr 14:35; 16:10, 15, this is the character of a good king, not of all kings.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. The interrogation in the affirmative strengthens the implied negation (compare Job 15:14; Ec 7:20).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. Various measures, implying that some are wrong (compare Pr 11:1; 16:11).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. The conduct of children even is the best test of principle (compare Mt 7:16).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. Hence, of course, God will know all you do (Psa 94:9).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. Activity and diligence contrasted with sloth (Pr 6:9; 10:11). lest ... poverty-- literally, "be deprived of inheritance."

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. when ... his way-- implying that he goes about boasting of his bargains.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. The contrast denotes the greater value of knowledge (compare Pr 3:14-16).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. Take his garment-- implies severe exaction, justified by the surety's rashness. a strange woman-- by some readings "strangers," but the former here, and in Pr 27:13, is allowable, and strengthens the sense. The debauchee is less reliable than the merely careless.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. Bread ... sweet-- either as unlawfully (Pr 9:17) or easily obtained. mouth ... gravel-- well expresses the pain and grief given at last.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. (Compare Pr 15:22). Be careful and considerate in important plans.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. Those who love to tell news will hardly keep secrets. flattereth ... lips-- (compare Margin; Pr 1:10). meddle ... him-- literally, "join," or "associate with."

entries.title: Song of Solomon 2:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. his lamp-- (Compare Pr 13:9; 24:20).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. gotten hastily-- contrary to God's providence (Pr 28:20), implying its unjust or easy attainment; hence the man is punished, or spends freely what he got easily (compare Pr 20:17).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. Man's goings-- literally, "Stately steppings of a strong man." a man-- any common man. understand-- or, "perceive."

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. devoureth ... holy-- or, better, "who rashly speaks promises," or "devotes what is holy," consecrating any thing. This suits better the last clause, which expresses a similar view of the results of rashly vowing.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. (Compare Pr 20:8). bringeth ... over them-- The wheel was used for threshing grain. The figure denotes severity (compare Am 1:3).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. The spirit ... Lord-- Men's minds are God's gifts, and thus able to search one another (compare Pr 20:5; Pr 18:8, 17; 1Co 2:11).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. The glory of young men ... the beauty of old men-- Each age has its peculiar excellence (Pr 16:31).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 30. blueness-- literally, "joining," the process of uniting the edges of a wound throws off purulent matter. stripes ... belly-- So punishment provides healing of soul (Pr 18:8), by deterring from evil courses.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 3:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 21 Pr 21:1-31. 1. rivers-- irrigating channels (Psa 1:3), whose course was easily turned (compare De 11:10). God disposes even kings as He pleases (Pr 16:9; Psa 33:15).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. high look-- (Compare Margin; Psa 131:1). proud heart-- or, "heart of breadth," one that is swollen (compare Psa 101:5). ploughing-- better "lamp," a frequent figure for prosperity (Pr 20:20); hence joy or delight.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. The contrast is between steady industry and rashness (compare Pr 19:2).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. The getting-- or, "what is obtained" (compare Job 7:2; Jr 22:13, Hebrew). vanity ... to and fro-- as fleeting as chaff or stubble in the wind (compare Pr 20:17-21; Psa 62:10). Such gettings are unsatisfactory. them ... death-- act as if they did (Pr 8:36; 17:19).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. robbery-- or, "destruction," especially oppression, of which they are authors. shall destroy-- literally, "cut with a saw" (1Ki 7:9), that is, utterly ruin them. Their sins shall be visited on them in kind. to do judgment-- what is just and right.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. of man-- any one; his way is opposed to truth, and also estranged from it. The pure proves himself such by his right conduct.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. corner-- a turret or arbor on the roof. brawling-- or contentious. wide house-- literally, "house of fellowship," large enough for several families.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. So strongly does he desire to do evil (Psa 10:3; Ec 8:11), that he will not even spare his friend if in his way.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. (Compare Pr 19:25). That which the simple learn by the terrors of punishment, the wise learn by teaching.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. (Compare Psa 37:35-38; 73:17, 20). house-- family or interests. overthroweth-- either supply "God" (compare Pr 10:24), or the word is used impersonally.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. The principles of retribution, often taught (compare Psa 18:26; Mt 7:1-12).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. The effect of bribery (Pr 17:23) is enhanced by secrecy, as the bribed person does not wish his motives made known.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. But the just love right and need no bribes. The wicked at last meet destruction, though for a time happy in concealing corruption.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. the way of understanding-- (Compare Pr 12:26; 14:22). remain-- that is, rest as at a journey's end; death will be his unchanging home.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. Costly luxuries impoverish.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 4:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. (Compare Pr 11:8). By suffering what they had devised for the righteous, or brought on them, the wicked became their ransom, in the usual sense of substitutes (compare Jos 7:26; Es 7:9).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. (Compare Pr 21:9). wilderness-- pasture, though uninhabitable ground (Psa 65:12).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. The wise, by diligence and care, lay up and increase wealth, while fools spend-- literally, "swallow it up," greedily.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. He who tries to act justly and kindly (Psa 34:14) will prosper and obtain justice and honor.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. "Wisdom is better than strength" (Ec 7:19; 9:15). strength ... thereof-- that in which they confide.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. The reproachful name is deserved by those who treat others with anger and contempt.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. desire-- that is, of ease and idleness brings him to starvation.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. The sin of covetousness marks the sluggard, as the virtue of benevolence the righteous.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. God regards the heart, and hypocrisy is more odious than open inconsistency. wicked mind-- or, "design" (Pr 1:4).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. (Compare Pr 19:5). that heareth-- or heeds instruction, and so grows wise. speaketh constantly-- or sincerely (compare Ha 1:5), and hence is believed (Pr 12:19; Jas 1:19).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. hardeneth his face-- is obstinate. directeth ... way-- considers it, and acts advisedly.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:11–12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 30-31. Men's best devices and reliances are vain compared with God's, or without His aid (Pr 19:21; Psa 20:7; 33:17).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 22 Pr 22:1-29. 1. A good name-- (Job 30:8, Hebrew); "good" is supplied here from Ec 7:1. loving favour-- kind regard, that is, of the wise and good.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. Before God all are on the same footing (Pr 14:31; 17:5).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 5:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. are punished-- that is, for their temerity; for the evil is not necessarily punitive, as the prudent might otherwise be its objects.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. humility and the fear of the Lord-- are in apposition; one produces the other. On the results, compare Pr 3:16; 8:18.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. he that ... them-- Those who properly watch over their own souls are thus preserved from the dangers which attend the way of perverse men (Pr 16:17).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. Train-- initiate, or early instruct. the way-- literally, "his way," that selected for him in which he should go; for early training secures habitual walking in it.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. The influence of wealth sets aside moral distinctions is implied, and, of course, disapproved (compare Pr 19:6; 21:14, &c.).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. (Compare Pr 11:18; Psa 109:16-20; Ga 6:7, 8). the rod ... fail-- His power to do evil will be destroyed.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. a bountiful eye-- that is, a beneficent disposition. for he giveth ... poor-- His acts prove it.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. Cast out-- or drive away. Scorners foster strife by taunts and revilings.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. (Compare Margin). pureness of heart-- and gentle, kind words win favor, even from kings.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. preserve-- or guard. knowledge-- its principles and possessors. overthroweth-- utterly confounds and destroys the wicked.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. Frivolous excuses satisfy the indolent man's conscience.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. The mouth-- or flattering speeches (Pr 5:3; 7:5) ensnare man, as pits, beasts. God makes their own sin their punishment.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. is bound-- or firmly fixed. Chastisement deters from crime and so leads to reformation of principle.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. These two vices pertain to the same selfish feeling. Both are deservedly odious to God and incur punishment.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 6:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. Here begins another division of the book, marked by those encouragements to the pursuit of wisdom, which are found in the earlier chapters. It will be observed that at Pr 22:22-24:12, the proverbs are generally expressed in two verses instead of one (see Introduction).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. These lessons must be laid up in the mind, and fitted-- or better, "fixed" in the lips so as to be ever ready.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. That ... Lord-- This is the design of the instruction.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. excellent things-- or probably of former times. counsels and knowledge-- both advice and instruction.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. Specially he desires to secure accuracy, so that his pupil may teach others.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:4–5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22-23. Here follow ten precepts of two verses each. Though men fail to defend the poor, God will (Pr 17:5; Psa 12:5). in the gate-- place of public gathering (Job 5:4; Psa 69:12).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:6–7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:8–9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. (Compare Pr 23:10). Do not entrench on others (De 19:14; 27:17).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. Success rewards diligence (Pr 10:4; 21:5).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 7:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 23 Pr 23:1-35. 1. Avoid the dangers of gluttony.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. put a knife-- an Eastern figure for putting restraint on the appetite.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. are deceitful meat-- though well tasted, injurious.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:2–3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4-5. (Compare 1Ti 6:9, 10). thine own wisdom-- which regards riches intrinsically as a blessing.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:4–6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6-8. Beware of deceitful men, whose courtesies even you will repent of having accepted. evil eye-- or purpose (Pr 22:9; De 15:9; Mt 6:23).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. (Compare Pr 9:8). "Cast not your pearls before swine" (Mt 7:6).

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:8–9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. Here begins another series of precepts.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:11–12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13-14. While there is little danger that the use of the "divine ordinance of the rod" will produce bodily harm, there is great hope of spiritual good.

entries.title: Song of Solomon 8:13–14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15-16. The pleasure afforded the teacher by the pupil's progress is a motive to diligence.
